feed.title: still
feed.subtitle: A minimal Zola theme focused on reading and writing.

entry.title: Why I removed the Home button
entry.content:
A quiet page does not need to explain itself too loudly.

When every screen includes a large cluster of controls, the reader is reminded that the interface matters as much as the text. I wanted the opposite feeling: arrive, read, leave with as little friction as possible.

Navigation should help the reader leave the chrome behind.

That is why this demo keeps navigation spare. Links remain available where they are useful, but they do not lead the page.

entry.title: Designing for less, not more
entry.content:
Minimal design is not the absence of decisions. It is the result of making many decisions and then refusing to display all of them at once.

In practice, that means:

  • choosing a comfortable line length
  • letting whitespace do structural work
  • using contrast sparingly
  • resisting components that call attention to themselves

A theme like still tries to make those choices feel inevitable rather than impressive.

entry.title: Notes on writing in public
entry.content:
Publishing online often tempts us to optimize for reaction instead of reflection.

This theme leans the other way. It favors a page that can hold a long paragraph, a small aside, or even a compact code block like this:

write clearly
remove noise
leave room

The goal is not austerity for its own sake. The goal is to keep attention close to the sentence.
